name: BRIXEQ
description: Tracker-grade step sequencer with per-step instrument snapshots. Every step on every track carries its own engine, envelope, filter, chord stack, send levels, probability, and trigger logic, no patches to switch between. Five concurrent tracks, sixteen patterns per track, sixteen scenes per project, plus a linear 64-row song mode. Six built-in engines (VA, FM, NOIZ, BD, SD, CY). Master delay + reverb + sidechain compressor. WAV recording to the SD card. Full manual at https://github.com/boorch/brixeq-pak/blob/main/MANUAL.md.
author: boorch (Bruce Menace/Cometary Trails)
features: Per-step instrument snapshots: each step is a full sound, not a parameter overlay
5 tracks × 16 patterns × 16 steps, plus a chord stack of up to 5 voices per step
16 scenes per project: pattern pointers, scale, transpose strips, master FX
Linear song mode (64 rows) with per-row scene reference + per-track overrides
Six engines: VA virtual analog, 2-op FM, noise generator, BD / SD / CY drums
Per-scene transpose sequencer with scale quantization
Master bus: tempo-synced ping-pong delay, stereo reverb, single-knob FAT compressor with optional sidechain
Step audition: tap B on the focused step to hear its full snapshot instantly
Realtime WAV recording (16-bit stereo 48 kHz) with auto-normalization to -3 dBFS
In-app help screen, project save / load with auto-load on boot
